evals.name: canonical-post-after-panel-review
evals.prompt:
I just got a panel-review back on PR 85 in catena-labs/example-repo (head SHA: abc123def456). Here are the findings:

Codex (MEDIUM, security)
- apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx:240 — External OAuth callbacks redirect to /v1/auth/oauth2/authorize while the post-signup onboarding gate is active. A new CLI/MCP magic-link signup can receive an access token without completing KYB.
  Fix: defer the non-console OAuth redirect until postSignupOnboardingActive is false.

Claude (LOW)
- apps/api/src/routes/organization.ts:33 — maxLength(80) runs against pre-trim string; trim should be inside the validator pipe.

Opencode (MEDIUM, deployment)
- apps/api/src/services/sign-up-provisioning.ts:37 — Drop of NODE_ENV === "production" from the BRIDGE_LIVE guard shifts behavior in staging.
  Fix: confirm staging keeps BRIDGE_LIVE=false or seed customer ID pre-KYB.

Please post these as inline review comments. For each one, walk me through it and I'll tell you what to do — but do NOT actually call gh api; this is a dry run. Write the final review payload to ./payload.json and a transcript of the triage to ./triage_transcript.md instead. For this dry run, treat my answers as: finding 1 (codex) = post as-is, finding 2 (claude) = make more concise, finding 3 (opencode) = soften the tone.
evals.expected_output: A payload.json containing exactly 3 inline comments (one per finding), each with: correct path/line, a two-line bolded header at the top of the body (`**Recommendation:** <Must fix|Should fix|Optional polish>` then `**Severity:** <High|Medium|Low>`) followed by a blank line and then the body text. Recommendation should be derived from severity when not explicitly tagged: HIGH→Must fix, MEDIUM→Should fix, LOW→Optional polish — so finding 1 (codex MEDIUM) gets `Should fix`, finding 2 (claude LOW) gets `Optional polish`, finding 3 (opencode MEDIUM) gets `Should fix`. Severity is rendered Title Case (`Medium`, `Low`), not the all-caps `MEDIUM`/`LOW` from the panelist tag. Each comment has the appropriate panelist's footer in <sub> tags linking to https://github.com/catena-labs/skills, event=COMMENT, commit_id=abc123def456. Finding 1's body text (after the header) should match the original codex text verbatim. Finding 2's body text should be shorter than the original claude text (concise rewrite). Finding 3's body text should be softened from the original opencode text (less imperative). HIGH-MEDIUM-LOW ordering: codex MEDIUM and opencode MEDIUM should appear before claude LOW. triage_transcript.md should summarize each disposition AND include a GitHub diff-view deep-link per finding in the form `https://github.com/catena-labs/example-repo/pull/85/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line>` (anchor uses sha256 of the file path). No actual gh api call is made. For each non-skipped finding, the transcript must also capture the exact AskUserQuestion `question` text the skill would have shown to the user — that text must self-contain the panelist+severity tag, the file:line, the deep-link URL on its own line (plain URL, not markdown link syntax), and the finding body verbatim, so the user wouldn't have to scroll back to chat to remember which finding the modal is asking about.

evals.name: delicate-tone-oss-contributor
evals.prompt:
PR 412 in some-oss-org/popular-lib (head SHA: 7f2e1a) is from a first-time external contributor. I ran a panel-review and got these findings — but the tone matters here, this person volunteered their time and we want them to come back. Findings:

Codex (MEDIUM, correctness)
- src/parser/tokenize.rs:88 — Unbounded recursion on nested groups. A pathological input like '((((...))))' x 10000 will overflow the stack.
  Fix: convert to an iterative loop with an explicit work stack.

Claude (LOW, style)
- src/parser/mod.rs:14 — Inconsistent error variant naming: ParseErr vs ParseError elsewhere in the file.

Opencode (MEDIUM, testing)
- tests/parser_test.rs:201 — New test only covers the happy path; the panic on invalid UTF-8 you fixed in this PR has no regression test.

Please help me triage and post these — but adapt the tone options for an external first-time contributor (encourage, frame as questions, thank them for the contribution). Dry-run: write the payload to ./payload.json, transcript to ./triage_transcript.md. My answers: post all three, but in the gentlest version of each — for the codex one, frame it as a question about edge cases rather than a demand; for the claude one, make it a tiny suggestion not a blocker; for the opencode one, thank them for the panic fix and ask if they could add a regression test.
evals.expected_output: payload.json with 3 inline comments. Each body opens with the two-line bolded header (`**Recommendation:** ...` then `**Severity:** ...`) before a blank line and the prose. Severity is Title Case. Recommendation is derived from severity (codex MEDIUM→`Should fix`, claude LOW→`Optional polish`, opencode MEDIUM→`Should fix`). The prose under the header should NOT read as terse imperatives — it should be visibly softer than the raw findings. Codex finding should be phrased as a question (e.g. 'Have you considered what happens with deeply nested input like...?' rather than 'Fix: convert to iterative'). Claude finding should be hedged ('small nit, feel free to ignore' or similar). Opencode finding should open with thanks for the panic fix and ask rather than demand. All three keep the file:line accurate and include the standard footer in <sub> tags. The skill should NOT have just used 'Soften the tone' mechanically four times — the rewrites should be situationally adapted (question / tiny-suggestion / thank-and-ask). triage_transcript.md should include a GitHub deep-link per finding (`https://github.com/some-oss-org/popular-lib/pull/412/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line>`). commit_id=7f2e1a, event=COMMENT. For each non-skipped finding, the transcript must also capture the exact AskUserQuestion `question` text the skill would have shown to the user — that text must self-contain the panelist+severity tag, the file:line, the deep-link URL on its own line (plain URL, not markdown link syntax), and the finding body verbatim, so the user wouldn't have to scroll back to chat to remember which finding the modal is asking about.

evals.name: finding-outside-diff-hunk
evals.prompt:
Post these panel-review findings to PR 27 in catena-labs/api-service (head SHA: 9c4b8e2). Dry-run mode — write to ./payload.json and ./triage_transcript.md, do not call gh api.

Codex (HIGH, security)
- src/auth/session.ts:142 — Session cookie missing SameSite=Strict; CSRF risk on the /admin endpoints.

Claude (MEDIUM)
- src/utils/logger.ts:8 — Logger swallows errors silently; should at least console.warn on serialization failure.

IMPORTANT context for the skill: src/utils/logger.ts:8 is on a line that is NOT part of the PR diff — it's pre-existing code that this PR didn't touch. The codex finding at src/auth/session.ts:142 IS on a changed line. GitHub will reject inline comments on lines outside the diff hunk. Please handle this correctly. My triage answers: post both as-is.
evals.expected_output: payload.json should contain BOTH comments (the skill cannot know in advance which lines GitHub will reject — it batches and lets the API tell it). Each body has the two-line bolded header at the top (`**Recommendation:** ...` then `**Severity:** ...` — codex HIGH→Must fix/High, claude MEDIUM→Should fix/Medium) followed by a blank line and the prose. Severity is Title Case in the header. triage_transcript.md should explicitly call out that src/utils/logger.ts:8 is likely to be rejected because it's outside the diff hunk, and recommend the user post it as a regular issue comment via `gh pr comment` if the API rejects it. Deep-link rules: the codex finding (changed line) gets a /pull/N/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line> diff-view link; the claude finding (outside hunk) gets the blob fallback `https://github.com/catena-labs/api-service/blob/9c4b8e2/src/utils/logger.ts#L8` because the diff anchor wouldn't scroll to anything useful. The skill must NOT silently drop the logger.ts finding before posting — that would hide a real review note. The codex HIGH finding must come first in payload.comments (severity ordering). Footer present on both. commit_id=9c4b8e2. For each non-skipped finding, the transcript must also capture the exact AskUserQuestion `question` text the skill would have shown to the user — that text must self-contain the panelist+severity tag, the file:line, the deep-link URL on its own line (plain URL, not markdown link syntax), and the finding body verbatim, so the user wouldn't have to scroll back to chat to remember which finding the modal is asking about.

evals.name: missing-pr-ref
evals.prompt:
I just got panel-review findings back. Please post them to GitHub. Findings:

Codex (MEDIUM)
- packages/db/src/migrations/0042_add_audit.sql:12 — NOT NULL added without a backfill default; existing rows will fail the migration.
  Fix: add DEFAULT now() or run a backfill in a separate migration first.

Claude (LOW)
- packages/db/src/schema.ts:88 — auditLog table defined but no index on user_id; queries by user will scan.

Dry-run mode: write to ./payload.json and ./triage_transcript.md. My answers (when you get to triaging): post both as-is.
evals.expected_output: The skill MUST NOT proceed to triage without first asking the user for the PR number or URL. There's no PR ref in the prompt and the skill's own guidance forbids auto-detecting from the current branch. Expected behavior: a clarifying question (asked once, ideally via AskUserQuestion or as a direct question in chat) requesting the PR ref. payload.json should NOT exist yet (or should exist but be empty / clearly marked 'awaiting PR ref'). The skill should NOT have run `gh pr view` against an inferred branch and should NOT have constructed a payload guessing at the repo. If the skill produced payload.json anyway, that's a fail.

evals.name: skip-all-low-bulk
evals.prompt:
Post these panel-review findings to PR 156 in catena-labs/web-app (head SHA: 4d8c1f9). Dry-run mode — payload to ./payload.json, transcript to ./triage_transcript.md, no gh api call.

Findings:

Codex (HIGH, security)
- apps/api/src/auth/jwt.ts:45 — JWT verification skipped when ENV.SKIP_AUTH=true; this flag exists in production env vars.
  Fix: remove the SKIP_AUTH bypass entirely or gate it on NODE_ENV !== 'production'.

Codex (HIGH, correctness)
- apps/api/src/billing/charge.ts:88 — Idempotency key derived from request timestamp; concurrent retries within the same millisecond will double-charge.
  Fix: derive the key from the request body hash or accept a client-supplied idempotency key.

Claude (MEDIUM)
- apps/web/src/components/Button.tsx:12 — Loading state has no aria-busy; screen readers won't announce the disabled state during async actions.

Opencode (MEDIUM, performance)
- apps/api/src/services/search.ts:201 — Sequential await in a loop over `users`; should be Promise.all if the calls are independent.
  Fix: Promise.all(users.map(getProfile)).

Claude (LOW)
- apps/web/src/utils/format.ts:7 — Date formatter doesn't handle null input; will crash with 'cannot read properties of null'.

Opencode (LOW)
- apps/api/src/routes/health.ts:4 — Health check returns 200 even when the DB connection is down.

Please post the HIGH and MEDIUM ones, skip the LOWs entirely — don't ask me about each LOW one individually. For the HIGH/MEDIUM ones, walk me through each: my answers are post-as-is for both HIGH codex findings, soften-the-tone for the claude MEDIUM (it's a small UX thing), and make-more-concise for the opencode MEDIUM.
evals.expected_output: payload.json has exactly 4 comments (2 HIGH + 2 MEDIUM, in that severity order: codex jwt HIGH, codex billing HIGH, claude aria MEDIUM, opencode search MEDIUM). Each body opens with the two-line bolded header (`**Recommendation:** ...` then `**Severity:** ...`) followed by a blank line and the prose. Recommendation is derived from severity (HIGH→Must fix, MEDIUM→Should fix). Severity is Title Case (`High`, `Medium`). The 2 LOW findings (format.ts and health.ts) must NOT appear in payload.comments. triage_transcript.md must list all 6 findings (including the skipped LOWs) with their dispositions and explicitly mark the two LOWs as 'skipped per bulk decision' (not 'asked user, user said skip'). Each of the 6 findings in the transcript should have a GitHub deep-link in the form `https://github.com/catena-labs/web-app/pull/156/files#diff-<sha256(path)>R<line>`. The aria-busy comment's prose (under the header) should be softer than the raw finding. The Promise.all comment's prose should be more concise than the raw finding. The two HIGH codex prose bodies should match the original text. All 4 have the footer. commit_id=4d8c1f9, event=COMMENT. For each non-skipped finding, the transcript must also capture the exact AskUserQuestion `question` text the skill would have shown to the user — that text must self-contain the panelist+severity tag, the file:line, the deep-link URL on its own line (plain URL, not markdown link syntax), and the finding body verbatim, so the user wouldn't have to scroll back to chat to remember which finding the modal is asking about.
